headline: CrowdStrike and Palo Alto post best quarter ever on AI cyber demand
detail: Both companies beat on revenue and profits, citing AI-driven threat escalation pulling forward enterprise security spend. This is a direct, positive fundamental catalyst for CRWD, which you hold at a 6.07% gain. The broader message is that AI tailwinds are real and accelerating for cybersecurity, which also supports sentiment across your tech holdings.
rec: You already own CRWD and it just had its catalyst - the move may be partly priced in now. Watch for a post-earnings fade before adding; if it holds above 720-730 on the next session, that confirms strength. No new add recommended until price settles.

headline: Brent posts biggest monthly oil decline since March 2020
detail: Collapsing oil prices reduce input costs broadly and take pressure off inflation expectations, which is a mild positive for growth and rate-sensitive tech names. Lower energy costs also help consumer spending, which supports the macro backdrop your tech book needs. ERY (inverse energy ETF) is on your scanner as a potential long - cheaper oil supports that directional trade.
rec: ERY shows a scanner WATCH setup (entry 12.93-12.95, stop 12.88) and the oil decline is a real catalyst backing the direction. Small speculative position only - it is a leveraged inverse ETF and you are already carrying margin risk across the book.

headline: Yen sinks to 40-year low, intervention risk elevated
detail: A weak yen historically pressures Japanese chipmakers and can ripple into global semi supply-chain sentiment, relevant to SMH and SOXL. More importantly, intervention risk means sudden sharp yen moves that can spike volatility across risk assets globally, which is a direct threat to leveraged positions like SOXL and SPAL.
rec: No trade action needed right now, but this is the macro wildcard that could shock your leveraged names on short notice. Keep stops on SOXL and SPAL respected - do not widen them because of this risk.

headline: AeroVironment skyrockets 19% on defense spending surge
detail: Defense drone demand is surging and the market is rewarding it aggressively. This does not directly touch your holdings but confirms that defense-tech is outperforming, which is a sector you have zero exposure to. No scanner setup in defense names aligns here for your current book.
rec: Interesting sector rotation signal but nothing in your holdings is affected and no scanner setup supports a new defense name buy today. Watch the trend - not a trade for right now.
